share.share_text: yaaaall good ð #paris

Being helivaced off of a mountaintop in the middle of a 16 mile circuit hike yesterday was NOT on my bucket list, but life comes at you fast ð

We're all safe and sound, and being trapped on a mountaintop cut off from any safe descent was a terrifying situation to find ourselves in. A massive thank you to @san_dimas_mrt for being absolute HEROES and so incredibly kind to us in the process. For more info, check the pinned comment and the image post after this.

I've always said I never want to do anything that would necessitate having SAR called on me, but as our rescuers put it, in the wilderness, things can spiral out of control fast. Pride should never get in the way of asking for help, and walking away from a dangerous situation alive and mostly unscathed is a victory. We are grateful to be alive. Onward and upward!
.
.
.
.
#hiking #hikingadventures #sar #searchandrescue #hikinglife

Follow me ð @historyinmemes 

Mongolian horse archery dates back over a thousand years, becoming a cornerstone of the Mongol Empire under Genghis Khan in the 13th century. Skilled warriors rode at full gallop, using powerful composite bows to strike enemies with deadly accuracy from horseback.

This combination of mobility and precision allowed Mongol armies to conquer vast territories across Asia and Europe. Today, Mongolian horse archery is preserved as a traditional sport and cultural practice, celebrated at festivals and competitions that honor the empireâs legendary cavalry heritage.

[via/ @carsrob ]

FLORIDA MAN ARRESTED AFTER ATTEMPTING TO TRADE HIS MOTHER-IN-LAW TO IRAN AS PART OF A HOSTAGE EXCHANGE PROGRAM HE MADE UP

Kissimmee, FL â A 36-year-old Kissimmee man is facing multiple charges after he contacted what he believed was an Iranian diplomatic channel via a WhatsApp number he found on a conspiracy forum and offered to exchange his mother-in-law for âtwo American hostages and a gas price reduction, negotiable.â
According to the arrest report, Carl Benitez drafted a three-page proposal he titled âUnofficial Hostage Exchange Offer â Serious Inquiries Onlyâ and sent it to four separate numbers, only one of which responded, and which turned out to be a marketing account for a Tehran-based carpet dealership.
The proposal included the following terms:

One (1) mother-in-law, described as âexperienced in conflict, very durable, speaks her mindâ
In exchange for: two unnamed American citizens currently in foreign detention
Plus: a 30-day freeze on gas prices at or below $3.10 per gallon at the Kissimmee Sunoco
A clause stipulating âshe comes back after six months if she wants but that part is her choiceâ
A signature line for âIranian Government Representativeâ and a notary block Benitez had pre-filled himself

When investigators traced the communications, Benitez told them he was acting as âan independent diplomatic contractorâ and asked if there was a finderâs fee for successful hostage exchanges. His mother-in-law, reached for comment by reporters, said she had heard about the offer and called it âtypical.â
The Tehran carpet dealership responded with a promotional offer for a hand-knotted 8x10. It declined to comment further.
Special Agent Omar Reyes stated, âHe pre-notarized the agreement. He had already signed his own name. He left the Iranian line blank because he said he wanted to give them the pen.â
He tried to trade his mother-in-law for hostages and a gas discount.
She heard about it. She called it typical. He is sleeping on the couch.
